academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent in a listed STEM field and must meet a programme-specific minimum final-mark threshold.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A minimum three-year undergraduate degree or equivalent in Biology, Biotechnology, Chemistry, Physics, Mathematics, Informatics, Statistics, or Engineering.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: The official programme page lists acceptable prior backgrounds in Biology, Biotechnology, Chemistry, Physics, Mathematics, Informatics, Statistics, or Engineering.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: The programme page requires a GPA or final bachelor's mark of at least 95/110, or the converted equivalent. Applicants must also verify the University of Padua's country-based specific entry title and GPA requirements.

accepted_proofs: Cambridge ESOL General and Higher Education certificates.
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if they obtained the entry qualification by completing their previous education entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from the countries listed by the University of Padua.

application_deadline_eu.notes: Call Three is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy. Applicants can apply for the degree programme only once per Academic Year.

application_deadline_non_eu.call_three: Not available for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Call Three is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy. Applicants can apply for the degree programme only once per Academic Year.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport or valid ID card
required_documents.notes: The official checklist allows a valid ID card for EU or equivalent applicants; other applicants normally provide a passport.
Required only for non-EU applicants already residing in Italy.
Documents must be official and written or translated in Italian or English.

required_documents.document_name: Academic qualification relevant for admission, if already obtained
required_documents.notes: The general University checklist requires the entry qualification document when already obtained.
Listed in the official general application checklist.
Listed in the official general application checklist.
Required unless the applicant qualifies for an official exemption.

required_documents.document_name: Other programme-specific documents required for the chosen degree programme
required_documents.notes: The University states that some programmes may require different or additional documents.

required_documents.document_name: Original and translated or legalised copies of previous qualifications
required_documents.notes: If admitted, applicants must later submit original qualification documents; no legalised documents are required at application stage.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No separate programme-specific entry exam stated
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Admission is based on the published degree, GPA, and language requirements; no written test, interview, or score threshold beyond the stated GPA rule is published on the official admissions pages.

source_quotes.quote: A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) in Biology, Biotechnology, Chemistry, Physics, Mathematics, Informatics, Statistics or Engineering.
A GPA or BA final mark of at least 95/110 (or converted equivalent)
A certificate B2 level (CEFR) or equivalent
Call Three: 2 June 2026 – 2 August 2026 (only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy)

uncertainty_notes: The official departmental QuaCBio page gives a programme-specific minimum final-mark requirement, while the apply.unipd.it page also refers applicants to the University's country-based entry-title and GPA rules; this JSON records both.
No separate programme-specific PDF admission notice was found beyond the official apply.unipd.it and QuaCBio programme pages reviewed on 2026-06-04.
